title: juniperus communis subsp. brevifolia
intro: Juniperus communis subsp. brevifolia, also known as Rocky Mountain juniper, is a subspecies of the juniper tree, characterized by its shorter stature and more compact growth habit compared to its counterpart, Juniperus communis subsp. communis. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Rocky Mountain juniper include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds, due to its antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to support digestive health, reduce anxiety, and promote relaxation.
